# @andes/progress-indicator-circular

<br />

There are two types of Progress Indicator Circular:

- indeterminate: internally controlled component which indicates loading.
- determinate: externally controlled component which gives more accurate information about the state of a process. For example: 25% loaded.

<br />

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Progress Indicator](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/7146d3-progress-indicator/b/26f535)_.

<br />

## How to use

<br />

<ProgressIndicatorCircular type="indeterminate" size="xsmall" color="inverse" />

<br />

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

npm install --save @andes/progress-indicator-circular

```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component with all the styles in your bundle:

```css

@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/index';

```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```css
/* Base */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';

/* By type */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/determinate';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/indeterminate';

/* By size */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/xsmall';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/small';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/medium';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/large';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/xlarge';

/* By modifier */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/modifier/inline';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/modifier/block';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/modifier/fullscreen';
```

> Always import `@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base` and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';

```

## Public API

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content. | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `color` | <sup>1</sup>Component fill color. | `string: 'basic', 'inverse'` | `'basic'` |  |
| `continuous` | If type is `determinate`, you can set `true` if your value is going to change continuously. Otherwise, if your progress value is going to change in a more "stepped" way, you can let this prop as false. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `id` | Component unique id. | `string` |  | |
| `label` | Label that describes the ProgressIndicatorCircular's contents | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `modifier` | <sup>2</sup>Alignment of the ProgressIndicatorCircular on the screen | `string: 'block', 'fullscreen', 'inline'` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Component size. | `string: 'xsmall', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'xlarge'` | `'medium'` |  |
| `srAnnouncement` | <sup>3</sup>Sr-only label that allows to notificate changes on the progress for screen reader users. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label that describes the ProgressIndicatorCircular's contents for screen readers. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `type` | Component variant type. | `string: 'determinate', 'indeterminate'` | `'indeterminate'` |  |
| `value` | If type is `determinate`, show element progress percentage. This can be between `0` and `100`.| `number` | `0` |  |

> 1 - `blue | white` values will be deprecated in a future major version. Please, use `basic` or `inverse` values instead.

> 2 - The modifier can be `block`: needs to be inside an element with `position: relative`, `fullscreen`: Renders in the middle of the screen, or `inline`: render as inline ProgressIndicatorCircular.

> 3 - Click [here](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/ARIA_Live_Regions) to read more about live regions

<br />

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Progress Indicator](http://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/progress-indicator-circular--andes-progress-indicator-circular-indeterminate).

### Basic example

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Basic.gif" alt="Basic Progress Indicator Circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
    id="my-progress-indicator"
    srLabel="Carga en proceso"
    srAnnouncement="Procesando..."
    />
  </>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
```

### With size

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Size.gif" alt="Size Progress Indicator Circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
    id="my-progress-indicator"
    srLabel="Carga en proceso"
    srAnnouncement="Procesando..."
    size="small"
    />
  </>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/styles/small';
```

### With color

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Color.gif" alt="Color Progress Indicator Circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div style={{ background: '#009EE3' }}>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
    id="my-progress-indicator"
    srLabel="Carga en proceso"
    srAnnouncement="Procesando..."
    color="inverse"
    />
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
```

### With label

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Label.gif" alt="Label Progress Indicator Circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
    id="my-progress-indicator"
    srLabel="Carga en proceso"
    srAnnouncement="Procesando..."
    label="Loading..."
    />
  </>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
```

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

Progress indicator circular allows modifying the following attributes:

| Attribute | Description                            |
| --------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `fill`    | Progress indicator circular fill color.  |
| `label`   | Progress indicator circular label color. |

Taking this into account, the `customTheme` object has the following structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    progressIndicator: {
      circular: {
        progress: {
          color: {
            fill: {
              [progressState]: fillColorValue,
            },
          },
        },
        color: {
          label: labelColorValue,
          fill: {
              [fillState]: fillColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

Possible `progressState` values:

```jsx

state: default | 'inverse'

```

Possible `fillState` values:

```jsx

state: base | 'inverse'

```

> For a more detailed view of the structure of this object you can refer to the file `@andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json`

#### Example

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Example-Reference-Theming.gif" alt="Reference Theming Progress indicator circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    progressIndicator: {
      circular: {
        progress: {
          color: {
            fill: {
              default: '#1B1E26',
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
      size="medium"
      srLabel="Downloading file"
      type="indeterminate"
    />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    progressIndicator: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes progress indicator regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

>[!WARNING]
> In this component the CSS styles will be applied to progress indicator, since it has a composed layout CSS properties functionality may not work for your use case.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

#### data-attributes

| data-attribute                                | Description                                                                                           |
| --------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-progress-indicator-circular`      | Progress indicator circular tag.                                                                      |
| `data-andes-progress-indicator-circular-size` | Progress indicator circular size. Can be: `'xsmall'`, `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'` or `'xlarge'`. |
| `data-andes-progress-indicator-circular-type` | Progress indicator circular type. Can be: `'indeterminate'` or `'determinate'`.                       |
| `data-andes-progress-indicator-circular-icon` | Progress indicator circular progress icon.                                                            |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/progress-indicator-circular/docs/Progress-Indicator-Circular-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.gif" alt="Style Overrides Theming Progress indicator circular Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ProgressIndicatorCircular } from '@andes/progress-indicator-circular';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    progressIndicator: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '[data-andes-progress-indicator-circular-icon]': {
          strokeWidth: '7px',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <ProgressIndicatorCircular
      size="medium"
      srLabel="Downloading file"
      type="indeterminate"
    />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
